mechanism_narrative: Monthly acceleration in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output that lifts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following above-median INDPRO changes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0% restricts credit formation and raises discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS > 4.0% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in the unemployment rate and the federal funds rate signal a stagflationary or restrictive-demand environment where both consumer spending and corporate borrowing costs deteriorate simultaneously, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The UNRATE-FEDFUNDS interaction term does not significantly predict negative 20-day returns (regression t-stat < -1.5) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment expansions during tight labor markets signal robust aggregate demand without slack-driven margin compression, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS surprises coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of tight-labor-market months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in real retail sales reflects resilient consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Monthly changes in RSAFS fail to achieve a rank correlation above 0.03 with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals broad-based output expansion that supports earnings growth and lifts S&P 500 valuations over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth does not exhibit positive rank correlation (IC < 0.03) with 60-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in consumer prices and retail sales indicate an overheating demand environment that pressures input costs and invites further monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction of month-over-month CPIAUCSL and RSAFS changes does not produce significantly negative 20-day returns (t-stat < -1.5) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month jump in the unemployment rate signals abrupt labor market deterioration that reduces household income and consumption forecasts, triggering rapid risk-off repricing in the S&P 500 over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are nonnegative in more than 50% of months where UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over a 2-year rolling window.
